reasoning: The United States conducted substantial airstrikes against Iran's nuclear facilities in late June 2025, which represents a major military escalation. However, the resolution criteria require a military offensive "intended to establish control over any portion of Iran," which implies a ground invasion or territorial annexation. Following the airstrikes, US Vice President JD Vance explicitly stated that the US has no interest in putting "boots on the ground" in Iran (June 2025), and President Trump announced a ceasefire between Israel and Iran shortly after. Since June, there have been no reports of a US ground mobilization or invasion plans. The Polymarket price was 0.03 on October 30, 2025. With less than two months remaining in the year as of the November 9 knowledge cutoff, a major US ground invasion of Iran to establish territorial control is highly improbable and logistics alone would require noticeable build-up that does not exist.
